header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Catalyst C9350 Series Smart Switches. Pass 1 of 2: this file carries the catalog (atomic SKU facts) and groups (named shared compatibility sets) — the reusable scaffolding. Switch models are added in Pass 2; until then 'models' is empty. All C9350 models are modular-uplink (uplink ports come from a fitted network module). Structure: catalog + groups + models.

sources.name: Cisco C9350 Series Smart Switches Data Sheet
Cisco C9350 Series Smart Switches Ordering Guide
Cisco Commerce (CCW) — operator-verified ordering (Meraki-classic license SKUs not in the public datasheet/ordering guide)

design_notes: Pass 1 of 2: catalog + groups only. 'models' is empty until Pass 2; the per-model validator checks (valid_primary subset, poe_budget_matrix max, access-port sum == total_port_count) therefore do not yet run. The standalone group-integrity and catalog port-speed checks DO run.
All C9350 models are modular-uplink: uplink ports come from a fitted network module (configurables.network_modules group). There are no fixed-uplink (inline uplink) models in this family.

C9350-NM-8Y is dual-mode: 8x 1G/10G/25G OR 4x 50G, mutually exclusive (top row to 50G, bottom row inactive). Modeled with the schema v0.7.0 `modes` concept: ONE orderable SKU, two alternative port configs; resolve.js uplinkOptions() expands each mode into a selectable option whose moduleId stays the real SKU.

Two license regimes apply: unified (LIC-CS-AC1-* — a single per-device subscription SKU; subscription term is variable, >=36 months, chosen at order rather than as a SKU suffix, so the unified license groups carry no term_variable) and meraki-classic (LIC-C9350-*-{3,5,7}Y term SKUs, term_variable choices_years [3,5,7]). There is NO -E/-A hardware split: the unified hardware offers both Essentials and Advantage; tier is a license choice (Pass-2 models will carry license_tier [essentials, advantage]).

Source discipline: hardware facts (modules, ports, PoE, PSU, cables) from the C9350 datasheet; SKU forms (PSU -I port-inlet suffix, unified license SKUs) from the ordering guide; the Meraki-classic license SKUs (LIC-C9350-*) are operator-verified in Cisco Commerce (CCW) and are not printed in the public datasheet or ordering guide.

Series-specific groups (PSU, stack/StackPower cables) are kept as their own named groups per project convention, even where a SKU resembles another series — the C9350 StackWise-1.6T and StackPower+ cables are explicitly NOT interchangeable with C9300 cables (datasheet Table 12).

catalog._comment: Atomic SKU facts. One row per real orderable component. What a thing IS, independent of which switch uses it.

network_modules.notes: Two 100G/40G QSFP28 uplinks, 200 Gbps total (datasheet p.25).
Four 100G/40G QSFP28 uplinks, 400 Gbps total (datasheet p.24). Per-model datasheet uplink lists: 48HX, 48TX, 24Y.

network_modules.description: Cisco C9350 8x 25G/10G/1G SFP28 (convertible to 4x 50G SFP56) network module

modes.name: 8x25G
modes.notes: Default mode: eight 1G/10G/25G SFP28 ports, 200 Gbps total (datasheet p.25).

modes.name: 4x50G
modes.notes: Converted mode: top row becomes four 50G SFP56 ports; bottom row set inactive (datasheet p.25).

network_modules.notes: Dual-mode, mutually exclusive (8x25G OR 4x50G). One orderable SKU C9350-NM-8Y; the mode is a configuration, not a separate SKU.
Eight 50G/25G/10G/1G SFP56 fiber uplinks, 400 Gbps total. Supported only on C9350-48HX and C9350-24Y (datasheet p.26).

network_modules.description: Cisco C9350 8x Multigigabit (10G/5G/2.5G/1G) RJ-45 network module
network_modules.notes: Eight 10G/5G/2.5G/1G RJ-45 Multigigabit uplinks, 80 Gbps total (datasheet p.26). Per-model datasheet uplink list: 48HM.

power_supplies.notes: 500W AC 80+ Platinum, Config-2 port-inlet (OG Table 4; datasheet Table 8).
850W AC 80+ Platinum, Config-2 port-inlet (OG Table 4; datasheet Table 8).
1600W AC 80+ Titanium, Config-2 port-inlet. Rated 1600W at 230V input / 1200W at 115V (datasheet Table 8; OG Table 4).

stack_cables.notes: StackWise-1.6T 50 cm stacking cable (OG Table 3; datasheet Table 12).

stackpower_cables.notes: StackPower+ 35 cm cable (OG Table 3; datasheet Table 12).
StackPower+ 100 cm cable (OG Table 3; datasheet Table 12).

licenses.notes: Cisco Switching Essentials License, Access Tier 1, Large (48-port). Single per-device unified subscription SKU; term variable (>=36 months) at order. OG Table 5.
Cisco Switching Advantage License, Access Tier 1, Large (48-port). OG Table 5.
Cisco Switching Essentials License, Access Tier 1, Medium (up to 24-port). OG Table 5.
Cisco Switching Advantage License, Access Tier 1, Medium (up to 24-port). OG Table 5.
Meraki-classic term license, Essentials, 48-port, 3-year. Operator-verified in CCW; not in public datasheet/OG.

groups._comment: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by id and inherits the whole set.

network_module_groups.notes: 200G-uplink models that take only the NM-2C and NM-8Y modules (datasheet per-model uplink lists): 24HX, 48HXN, 48U, 24U, 48P, 24P, 48T, 24T, 12Y, 48S, 24S. NM-8Y contributes either 8x25G or 4x50G via its modes.
Adds the 400G NM-4C: C9350-48TX (datasheet p.17 lists NM-4C/2C/8Y, no NM-8L). 48HX also takes NM-4C but additionally takes NM-8L, so it uses the c9350-8l group.

Models that take the NM-8L (50G fiber): C9350-48HX and C9350-24Y. Datasheet p.26 states NM-8L is 'supported only on the C9350-24Y and C9350-48HX SKUs'; both also list NM-4C. (The 48HX model page p.16 omits NM-8L; the NM-8L module page is taken as authoritative per project ruling — datasheet wins, including not-yet-orderable models.)
C9350-48HM, which adds the mGig copper NM-8M (datasheet p.18 lists NM-2C/8Y/8M).

power_supply_groups.notes: Cisco C9350 Config-2 AC PSU family (datasheet Table 8 / OG Table 4, -I port-inlet SKUs). The chassis has 3 PSU bays (N+1); which member is a valid PRIMARY, the default shipped PSU, and the PoE budget per PSU combination are switch-specific and live on the model entry (Pass 2). Excludes ordering variants (-IUP primary-upgrade, /2 redundant, = spare).

stack_cable_groups.notes: StackWise-1.6T Type-1A data-stack cables (datasheet Table 12 / OG Table 3). Datasheet Table 12 note: not interchangeable with C9300-series stacking cables.

stackpower_cable_groups.notes: StackPower+ cables (datasheet Table 12 / OG Table 3). Datasheet Table 12 note: not interchangeable with C9300-series StackPower cables.

license_groups.applies_to: Large (48-port) C9350 models, Essentials tier, unified subscription.
Large (48-port) C9350 models, Advantage tier, unified subscription.
Medium (up to 24-port, incl. 12-port) C9350 models, Essentials tier, unified subscription.
Medium (up to 24-port, incl. 12-port) C9350 models, Advantage tier, unified subscription.
48-port C9350 models, Essentials tier, Meraki-classic term license.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

license_groups.applies_to: 48-port C9350 models, Advantage tier, Meraki-classic term license.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

license_groups.applies_to: 24-port (and 12-port) C9350 models, Essentials tier, Meraki-classic term license.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

license_groups.applies_to: 24-port (and 12-port) C9350 models, Advantage tier, Meraki-classic term license.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE; the pick resolves the concrete -<n>Y subscription SKU.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst C9350-24HX, 24x mGig (1/2.5/5/10G), upoe+ (2160W budget), modular uplinks. Unified + Meraki-classic licensing, both tiers.

axis_values._comment: Registry-axis values this switch supplies to the solver.

axis_value_provenance._comment: How resolved values were derived (for re-validation).
axis_value_provenance.ports: Datasheet per-model page port speeds + Table 6. 100M/10M are display-only, not filter speeds.
axis_value_provenance.poe_budget_watts: Datasheet model-page total PoE budget; matrix from Table 9.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + per-model PSU/PoE overlay.

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: Datasheet Table 9 (PoE budget per PSU combination: single primary, +secondary, and +tertiary where a third PSU is needed to reach the rated budget).

included_by_default_non_selectable._comment: Shipped by default, not separately selectable (OG section 2).
